Title: An R2R3-type MYB transcription factor, GmMYB77, negatively regulates isoflavone accumulation in soybean [Glycine max (L.) Merr.]

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB family transcription factor with R2R3 repeat domain
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression in hairy roots, tobacco leaves, and stable soybean OE lines; native GmMYB77 promoter haplotypes (Hap-P1, Hap-P2, Hap-P3) tested in promoter activity assays; Arabidopsis RPS5A promoter for Cas9 and Arabidopsis U6 promoter for sgRNA in CRISPR/Cas9 knockout
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytoestrogen beneficial to human health, including reduced breast cancer risk and alleviation of menopausal syndrome symptoms; contributes to plant defence, with malonylglycitin implicated in bitterness and pathogen-related defence metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seeds; also measured in hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: isoflavone biosynthesis and pathway flux partitioning within phenylpropanoid/flavonoid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: negative regulator and major controller of malonylglycitin and total isoflavone accumulation; promoter haplotype Hap-P2 is an elite low-expression allele associated with high isoflavone content
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: direct transcriptional repression via promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses GmIFS1, GmIFS2, GmCHS7, and GmCHS8; also associated with suppression of GmIOMT1 and likely GmF6H; overexpression reduced GmCHR2 and GmCHR3, whereas loss/silencing increased GmPAL1, Gm4CL, and GmCHR6 expression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Natural promoter variation/haplotype differences (especially Hap-P2) affecting GmMYB77 expression
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GmIFS1 promoter; GmIFS2 promoter; GmCHS7 promoter; GmCHS8 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all soybean organs, highest in roots and lower in seeds
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in all soybean organs, higher in roots and lower in seeds; Hap-P2 accessions show lowest GmMYB77 expression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown plants across multiple environments (Beijing, Hainan, Anhui; 2017-2018) for haplotype-trait associations
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses malonylglycitin and total isoflavone accumulation; knockout/RNAi increases isoflavone content, while overexpression decreases it
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant differences detected in plant height, first pod height, node number, effective branches, seed weight per plant, seed number per plant, or 100-seed weight between knockout, overexpression, and TL1 plants
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for soybean quality improvement: select elite promoter haplotype Hap-P2 by marker-assisted selection or knock out/edit GmMYB77 via CRISPR/Cas9 to raise malonylglycitin and total isoflavone without agronomic penalty; useful molecular marker resource for breeding
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA-seq; GWAS; soybean hairy-root overexpression and RNAi; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in stable soybean; stable overexpression in soybean; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling by HPLC; dual-luciferase assays; EMSA; yeast one-hybrid; promoter haplotype activity assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: BSA-seq using pooled whole-genome sequencing on Illumina HiSeq X Ten; GWAS using 6,149,599 SNPs from 2,214 resequenced soybean accessions (from previous studies); no accession number reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GmMYB77 directly binds the GGT core sequence. Coding-region haplotypes Hap-C1 and Hap-C2 did not show clear functional differences in repression activity, whereas promoter haplotypes strongly affected expression and metabolite accumulation. Hap-P2 had the lowest promoter activity and highest malonylglycitin/TIF. The study emphasizes stronger effects on malonylglycitin than on total isoflavone and notes tissue differences between hairy roots and seeds.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMYB77 was identified by combined BSA-seq and GWAS as a major locus associated with soybean malonylglycitin/isoflavone variation. 2. GmMYB77 functions as a negative regulator: overexpression decreased daidzin, malonylglycitin, malonyldaidzin, and total isoflavone in hairy roots and reduced malonylglycitin, malonylgenistin, and total isoflavone in seeds; RNAi or CRISPR knockout increased these metabolites. 3. GmMYB77 directly binds a GGT core motif in the promoters of GmIFS1, GmIFS2, GmCHS7, and GmCHS8 and represses their transcription, explaining reduced isoflavone biosynthetic flux. 4. Natural variation in the promoter, especially Hap-P2, lowers GmMYB77 expression and is associated with significantly higher malonylglycitin and total isoflavone, making it an elite breeding haplotype.
